skill.name: template-skill
skill.description: A minimal starter scaffold for defining a new AI skill with required metadata and instruction space.
skill.summary: Start a new skill from a simple, documented SKILL.md template.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: This official skill contains only a minimal SKILL.md template with placeholder metadata and no executable code. Static analysis found no scripts, network access, filesystem operations, environment access, or external command use.

content.value_statement: Creating a new AI skill requires a consistent metadata and instruction structure. This template gives authors a simple SKILL.md starting point for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Provides a minimal SKILL.md file with frontmatter metadata fields.
Shows where to define the skill name and description.
Provides a clear location for adding task-specific instructions.
Serves as a neutral base for custom skill development.
Avoids scripts, assets, dependencies, and executable behavior.
limitations: Does not include domain-specific instructions or examples.
Does not provide automation scripts, tools, or helper files.
Requires authors to replace placeholder text before practical use.
Does not validate completed skills or marketplace metadata.

use_cases.title: Start a New Skill
use_cases.description: Use the template as the first SKILL.md file for a new custom skill.

use_cases.title: Teach Skill Structure
use_cases.description: Show new contributors the minimum metadata and instruction layout for a skill.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Drafts
use_cases.description: Give teams a common starter file before they add specialized instructions and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the template skill to draft a new SKILL.md for a skill that helps with meeting notes.

prompt_templates.title: Replace Placeholder Metadata
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the template with a clear name and description for a data-cleaning skill.

prompt_templates.title: Add Practical Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the template structure and add concise instructions for when the skill should activate.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Marketplace Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this template into a complete skill draft with metadata, scope, limitations, and usage examples.

output_examples.input: Create a starter skill for research summaries.
output_examples.output: A short skill draft with a clear name, a focused description, and instructions for summarizing research sources.

output: A section where authors add operating instructions.

output_examples.input: Improve this placeholder skill for publication.
output_examples.output: A revised draft that replaces placeholder text with specific purpose, activation guidance, and practical boundaries.

best_practices: Replace all placeholder text before using the skill in production.
Write a description that states when the skill should be used.
Keep instructions specific, testable, and limited to the skill purpose.
anti_patterns: Do not publish the template without replacing the placeholder description.
Do not add broad instructions that apply to every task.
Do not include secrets, credentials, or private system details in SKILL.md.

faq.question: What does this skill provide?
faq.answer: It provides a minimal SKILL.md template for creating a new AI skill.

faq.question: Is this a finished functional skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a starter template that authors must customize before use.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or commands?
faq.answer: No. The source contains only a Markdown template file.

faq.question: Who should use this template?
faq.answer: Skill authors, documentation teams, and AI platform teams can use it to start drafts.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code support.

faq.question: What should I add before publishing?
faq.answer: Add a specific description, clear activation guidance, examples, limitations, and any needed supporting files.
